aliases: pin the bar before the agent builds
hard-gate floor plus opt-in recalled lessons
reinforce included lessons, decay dropped ones
close the build loop back into agent memory
file a failure lesson under a topic node
blurb: Wiring `regin goal preflight`/`feedback`, the hard-gate floor, or how a goal run pins the bar and feeds memory.
intent: The two regin-side halves of the `/goal-verified` loop-engineering workflow (the build + verify halves are the skill). Preflight is a deterministic, offline kernel: it assembles a roadmap of the universal hard-gate floor (tests stay green + an independent fresh-context reviewer) plus, opt-in, a short menu of FTS-recalled past lessons — pinning a falsifiable bar before code is written. Per-area convention skills and reference components are sourced outside this kernel (the file-keyed table in CLAUDE.local.md and the real target files). Feedback closes the loop after verification, reusing memory primitives only: included lessons are reinforced, FAILED acceptance items become area-tagged `goal-verified-fail` lesson memories, and those failure-lessons are filed under agent-supplied authoritative topic nodes.
label: Loop-engineering: goal preflight roadmap → verified build → feedback into memory
